perturbation.original_text: (3) "Product" means any computer or related hardware and peripherals (including hubs, switches and routers or networking hardware) or software products (including networking software products) which CompuCom has the ability to sell.
perturbation.changed_text: (3) "Product" means any item CompuCom decides to include. This may or may not encompass any computer or related hardware and peripherals (including hubs, switches and routers or networking hardware) or software products (including networking software products).
perturbation.explanation: Altering the definition of "Product" to be determined solely at CompuCom's discretion introduces ambiguity. It now contradicts Article III sections that clearly define obligations related to "Products", as the definition is no longer clear, so obligations are therefore unclear. This creates uncertainty about what is subject to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.1 (b)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the United States to comply with Seller's obligations under Seller's international alliance agreements and (C) to perform initial on-site image load/configuration and augmentation in a manner consistent with Seller's current business practices, provided that Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will not actively encourage any Services customer to move its initial image load/configuration business from CompuCom's configuration facilities to the customer`s site.
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of "Product" gives CompuCom the sole discretion to decide what constitutes a "Product". This contradicts the contradicted text which defines specific obligations related to the procurement of "Products" from CompuCom based on the original definition of 'Product' which includes "any computer or related hardware and peripherals". Now that CompuCom decides, the contradicted text is uncertain and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (6) "Service Accounts" means the customer accounts of Seller listed on Exhibits B and C hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) "Service Accounts" means the customer accounts of Seller, which may or may not be listed on Exhibits B and C hereto, as determined by CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of "Service Accounts" to be determined by CompuCom, regardless of the exhibits, introduces a contradiction. It contradicts Article III 3.2(a)(i) and (ii) as the exhibits mentioned on those clauses are relevant to "Service Accounts" and that are no longer reliable. The change creates uncertainty about which accounts are subject to the restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.2 (a)
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 CompuCom's Agreement. (a) CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; (ii) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit C hereto except to the extent provided for in such Exhibit; or (iii) except as contemplated by the Asset Purchase Agreement, solicit, entice or induce any employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller to terminate his or her employment with Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller. Notwithstanding the foregoing, CompuCom shall be permitted to provide Services under any "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work submitted by CompuCom to the applicable potential customer prior to May 12, 1999 . In the event of a dispute between CompuCom and Seller as to the matters covered by the immediately preceeding sentence, CompuCom shall furnish, in response to the reasonable request of Seller, evidence and shall have the burden of proving, that the applicable "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work was submitted by CompuCom to the customer prior to May 12, 1999.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Service Accounts" based on Exhibits B and C. The changed text makes the definition of "Service Accounts" dependent on CompuCom's determination, regardless of the exhibits. This directly contradicts section 3.2 which refers to Exhibits B and C for defining CompuCom's non-compete obligations. The change introduces uncertainty and inconsistency because the exhibits are no longer the sole determinant of which accounts are subject to the non-compete restrictions, potentially rendering the non-compete agreement unenforceable regarding specific accounts. The contradiction arises because the exhibits are relevant to 'Service Accounts' in 3.2, as the exhibits mentioned on those clauses are relevant to 'Service Accounts', and making the exhibits unreliable by allowing CompuCom to redefine the term.

perturbation.original_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom.
perturbation.changed_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it may or may not accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b). The decision to accept orders will be at CompuCom's sole discretion, regardless of the conditions listed below: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing the phrase 'may or may not accept orders...at CompuCom's sole discretion, regardless of the conditions', the modified text directly contradicts the rest of Article IV, which outlines specific conditions under which CompuCom *will* accept orders. Now, even if all listed conditions are met, CompuCom can arbitrarily reject orders, rendering those conditions meaningless and unenforceable. This creates in-text contradiction by both stating conditions for acceptance and simultaneously nullifying their importance.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders
perturbation.contradicted_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it may or may not accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b). The decision to accept orders will be at CompuCom's sole discretion, regardless of the conditions listed below: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom. CompuCom agrees to notify <PAGE> Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product within a mutually agreed upon time that is reasonable under the time constraints required by the customer order or contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it states CompuCom 'may or may not accept orders...at CompuCom's sole discretion, regardless of the conditions', which directly clashes with the initial agreement in both the original text and the contradicted text. The original text's conditions under which CompuCom *will* accept orders are rendered meaningless and unenforceable by the change, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The changed and contradicted texts are actually the same, however the change from the original to changed causes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Seller covenants and agrees that, unless otherwise required by law, from and after the Closing: (a) Seller shall cooperate with CompuCom at CompuCom's expense to protect and safeguard all of CompuCom's Confidential Information; and (b) Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, the terms "CompuCom's Confidential Information" means proprietary or confidential information concerning the Business including, without limitation, information regarding prices charged for Products, the assets, liabilities, and financial condition of the Business, names and identities of customers and analyses of the amount and types of Products purchased by each such customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to disclose historical financial information, including financial information relating to the Business, as may be required by customers, vendors, lenders or other third parties, provided that such third parties shall agree to preserve the confidentiality of such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller covenants and agrees that, unless otherwise required by law, from and after the Closing: (a) Seller shall cooperate with CompuCom at CompuCom's expense to protect and safeguard all of CompuCom's Confidential Information; and (b) Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information, except when Seller deems it necessary for business purposes. As used in this Agreement, the terms "CompuCom's Confidential Information" means proprietary or confidential information concerning the Business including, without limitation, information regarding prices charged for Products, the assets, liabilities, and financial condition of the Business, names and identities of customers and analyses of the amount and types of Products purchased by each such customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to disclose historical financial information, including financial information relating to the Business, as may be required by customers, vendors, lenders or other third parties, provided that such third parties shall agree to preserve the confidentiality of such information.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except when Seller deems it necessary for business purposes' to 5.1(b) directly contradicts the preceding statement that 'Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information'. It introduces an exception that gives Seller broad discretion to disclose confidential information, undermining the original non-disclosure obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller covenants and agrees that, unless otherwise required by law, from and after the Closing: (a) Seller shall cooperate with CompuCom at CompuCom's expense to protect and safeguard all of CompuCom's Confidential Information; and (b) Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information, except when Seller deems it necessary for business purposes. As used in this Agreement, the terms "CompuCom's Confidential Information" means proprietary or confidential information concerning the Business including, without limitation, information regarding prices charged for Products, the assets, liabilities, and financial condition of the Business, names and identities of customers and analyses of the amount and types of Products purchased by each such customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to disclose historical financial information, including financial information relating to the Business, as may be required by customers, vendors, lenders or other third parties, provided that such third parties shall agree to preserve the confidentiality of such information.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'except when Seller deems it necessary for business purposes' to the clause prohibiting the use or disclosure of CompuCom's Confidential Information introduces a significant contradiction. Previously, the Seller was strictly prohibited from using or disclosing such information. The added exception gives the Seller broad discretion, directly conflicting with the original prohibition. The contradiction lies in the fact that the seller now has the power to determine when such use or disclosure is acceptable, undermining the confidentiality obligation and leading to uncertainty regarding what constitutes a breach of contract. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because a clause is modified to have a conflicting implication.
